lens: Cross-sector deal flow stays heavy on a risk-on close (Russell 2000 +1.5%, Nasdaq +1.3%): Prologis' ~£14B "best and final" run at SEGRO (JPMorgan, BofA), NOVAGOLD's ~$4.2B Donlin Gold consolidation (Citi), Vertex's $85/share take-private of Crinetics (JPMorgan, Leerink; Lazard, Morgan Stanley), and the Corebridge/Equitable merger of equals (Morgan Stanley; Goldman) heading to a Jul 30 vote.

stories.headline: JPMorgan and BofA advise Prologis on its ~£14B ($18.7B) "best and final" bid for SEGRO
stories.why_it_matters: Prologis made a "best and final" ~£14B all-share (plus partial-cash) proposal for UK logistics REIT SEGRO and sought a PUSU deadline extension. The release names J.P. Morgan (J.P. Morgan Cazenove) and Merrill Lynch International (BofA Securities), alongside Rothschild and Eastdil, as acting exclusively for Prologis (buyer). A contested UK-Takeover-Code mega-cap with a multi-bank syndicate drives enormous cross-border diligence.
stories.suggested_action: Engage JPMorgan's real estate / cross-border M&A coverage on Prologis/SEGRO — a contested UK-Code mega-cap is a heavy multi-party, multi-jurisdiction data-room use case.

deal_clock.event: Prologis makes "best and final" ~£14B proposal for SEGRO

stories.headline: JPMorgan (with Leerink) advises Crinetics on its $85/share, all-cash sale to Vertex; Lazard and Morgan Stanley advise Vertex
stories.why_it_matters: Vertex Pharmaceuticals agreed to acquire Crinetics Pharmaceuticals for $85.00/share in cash (raised from $78.00). The preliminary proxy names J.P. Morgan and Leerink Partners as financial advisers to Crinetics (target, each delivering a fairness opinion) and Lazard and Morgan Stanley as advisers to Vertex (buyer), with a three-bank debt commitment. A sizable, financed biopharma take-private now advancing to the vote.
stories.suggested_action: Open with JPMorgan's healthcare coverage on Crinetics/Vertex — a financed all-cash biopharma take-private runs heavy, time-boxed diligence through the shareholder vote.

deal_clock.event: Crinetics files preliminary proxy; $85.00/share cash from Vertex

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley advises Corebridge and Goldman advises Equitable on their merger of equals
stories.why_it_matters: Corebridge Financial and Equitable Holdings are combining in an all-stock merger of equals under a new holding company ("New Equitable"), with Corebridge holders ending up with ~51%. A supplemental joint-proxy disclosure filed Jul 21 names Morgan Stanley as Corebridge's financial adviser and Goldman Sachs as Equitable's, ahead of a Jul 30 special meeting. A large-cap insurance MOE with two-sided fairness work heading into the vote.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Morgan Stanley's FIG coverage on the Corebridge/Equitable MOE — a large insurance combination with an imminent vote runs multi-workstream integration diligence.

deal_clock.event: Supplemental joint-proxy disclosure filed
Special meeting / shareholder vote

stories.headline: Citi advises NOVAGOLD on its ~$4.2B all-share acquisition of 100% of Donlin Gold
stories.why_it_matters: NOVAGOLD entered definitive agreements to acquire Paulson's 40% interest in Donlin Gold in an all-share deal implying ~US$4.2B combined equity value. The release names Citi as exclusive financial adviser to NOVAGOLD, also delivering a fairness opinion to its board (dated Jul 21). A JV-consolidation of scale with board-diligence and shareholder-vote document flow.
stories.suggested_action: Engage Citi's metals & mining coverage on NOVAGOLD/Donlin — a $4.2B all-share consolidation with a board fairness opinion is a strong secure-diligence use case.

deal_clock.event: NOVAGOLD signs definitive agreements to acquire 100% of Donlin Gold

stories.headline: Goldman and Perella Weinberg advise Repligen on its cash-and-stock acquisition of BioLife Solutions; Centerview advises BioLife
stories.why_it_matters: Repligen agreed to acquire BioLife Solutions for $11.25 cash plus 0.1442 Repligen shares per BioLife share (expected close Q4 2026). The Form 425 names Goldman Sachs and Perella Weinberg as advisers to Repligen (buyer) and Centerview Partners as adviser to BioLife (target). A mid-cap life-sciences-tools combination with two-sided diligence as the S-4 process begins.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Goldman's healthcare/life-sciences coverage on Repligen/BioLife — a cash-and-stock strategic acquisition spins up two-sided diligence into the S-4.

deal_clock.event: Repligen agrees to acquire BioLife Solutions (cash + stock)

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley and Wells Fargo advise Greenbacker on its ~$375M sale to MN8 (JPMorgan advises MN8)
stories.why_it_matters: MN8 Energy agreed to acquire Greenbacker Renewable Energy for ~$375M, creating a top-tier US power platform. The release names Morgan Stanley and Wells Fargo as advisers to Greenbacker (seller) and J.P. Morgan as adviser to MN8 (buyer). A renewables consolidation with portfolio-asset and PPA/contract diligence across shared rooms.
stories.suggested_action: Talk to Morgan Stanley's power & renewables coverage on the Greenbacker sale — renewables platform deals run complex contract and asset diligence.

deal_clock.event: MN8 agrees to acquire Greenbacker for ~$375M

stories.headline: Jefferies, TD Cowen, Stifel and Guggenheim place Mentari Therapeutics' $200M PIPE (ahead of an InMed reverse merger)
stories.why_it_matters: Mentari Therapeutics announced a $200M private placement for its migraine-prevention pipeline, concurrent with a reverse merger into InMed Pharmaceuticals. The release names Jefferies, TD Cowen, Stifel and Guggenheim Securities as placement agents. A large PIPE plus a public-shell reverse merger drives confidential financing diligence across four agents.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Jefferies's healthcare ECM/private-capital coverage on the Mentari PIPE — a $200M placement plus reverse merger runs document-intensive investor diligence.

deal_clock.event: Mentari announces $200M private placement + InMed reverse merger

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley, Jefferies, Evercore and Raymond James run Dyne Therapeutics' upsized $375M offering
stories.why_it_matters: Dyne Therapeutics priced an upsized $375M public offering (18.3M shares at $20.50). The release names Morgan Stanley, Jefferies and Evercore ISI as joint book-running managers, with LifeSci Capital and Raymond James also joint book-running managers. A large upsized equity raise across multiple banks means active ECM underwriting and closing diligence (expected Jul 23).
stories.suggested_action: Use the Dyne raise as an ECM hook with Evercore's (and the syndicate's) healthcare capital-markets desks — upsized follow-ons run coordinated underwriting diligence to close.

deal_clock.event: Dyne prices upsized $375M offering at $20.50
Expected closing

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley advises First Financial Bancorp on its acquisition of Finward Bancorp
stories.why_it_matters: First Financial Bancorp (Ohio) agreed to acquire Finward Bancorp (Indiana) in a stock-based bank merger (Finward merges into First Financial). The Form 425 names Morgan Stanley as financial adviser to First Financial (buyer) and Stephens as adviser to Finward (seller). A community-bank combination advancing to an S-4 and shareholder vote.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Morgan Stanley's depositories/FIG coverage on First Financial/Finward — a signed bank merger runs two-sided diligence into the S-4 and vote.

deal_clock.event: First Financial agrees to acquire Finward Bancorp

stories.headline: Perella Weinberg (TPH&Co) advises Citadel Drilling on its US$65M sale to Ensign Energy Services
stories.why_it_matters: Ensign Energy Services agreed to acquire Citadel Drilling for US$65M. The release names TPH&Co, the energy business of Perella Weinberg Partners, as financial adviser to Citadel (seller). A cross-border oilfield-services sell-side with a single adviser is a targeted, faster-close diligence engagement.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Perella Weinberg's (TPH&Co) energy coverage on the Citadel sale — a focused oilfield-services sell-side is a clean, near-term diligence conversation.

deal_clock.event: Ensign agrees to acquire Citadel Drilling for US$65M

stories.headline: Lincoln International advises Senti Biosciences' Special Committee on its CVR-only take-private by Celadon
stories.why_it_matters: Celadon Partners (an affiliate of Senti's largest stockholder) agreed to take Senti Biosciences private, with consideration consisting solely of contingent value rights capped at $60M (no upfront cash for common stock). The preliminary proxy names Lincoln International as financial adviser to Senti's Special Committee, delivering the fairness opinion. A distressed, special-committee-led take-private — process-heavy despite its small size.
stories.suggested_action: A special-committee CVR-only take-private is a niche but document-heavy process — worth a light-touch note to Lincoln International on secure special-committee diligence.

deal_clock.event: Senti files preliminary proxy for CVR-only take-private by Celadon

stories.headline: Risk-on rally holds into the close: Nasdaq +1.3%, S&P +0.9%, Russell 2000 leads +1.5%, VIX ~17
stories.why_it_matters: US equities closed broadly higher on Jul 21, with the small-cap Russell 2000 leading (+1.5%) — signaling participation beyond mega-cap tech — and the VIX falling ~8.6% to ~17.1. Advisory names closed up with the tape (GS +2.9%, MS +2.6%, EVR +2.1%, JEF +2.8%). A constructive backdrop is where fresh mega-deals (Prologis/SEGRO, NOVAGOLD/Donlin) and financings (Dyne, Mentari) get signed and launched.
stories.suggested_action: Frame outreach around a risk-on tape supporting fresh signings and raises — a natural reason to talk room readiness as deal flow accelerates.

opportunities.why_it_matters: J.P. Morgan and Leerink advise Crinetics on its signed $85.00/share all-cash sale to Vertex (Lazard and Morgan Stanley advise Vertex), with a three-bank debt commitment. A sizable, financed biopharma take-private advancing to the shareholder vote.

deal_clock.event: Crinetics files preliminary proxy; $85.00/share cash from Vertex

market_snapshot.macro_note: Broad risk-on rally at the Jul 21 close: S&P +0.9% (7,509), Nasdaq +1.3% (25,837), Dow +0.7%, with the small-cap Russell 2000 leading (+1.5%) and the VIX down ~8.6% to ~17.1 — participation beyond mega-cap tech. Advisory names closed higher (GS +2.9%, MS +2.6%, EVR +2.1%, JEF +2.8%). Values are Jul 21 closing levels; WTI omitted (only a Jul 22 intraday tick was verifiable) and no single macro catalyst was independently confirmable — accuracy over completeness.

talking_point.quote: The tape keeps handing bankers reasons to open a room — Prologis' £14B run at SEGRO, NOVAGOLD's $4.2B Donlin consolidation, Vertex taking Crinetics private at $85, and the Corebridge/Equitable merger heading to a Jul 30 vote — all on a risk-on session led by small caps. Every one of these spins up diligence the day it signs. How are you thinking about room readiness across the processes you're launching this quarter?
talking_point.use_with: M&A, FIG, energy, and healthcare coverage teams (esp. JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, Goldman, Citi, Jefferies, Evercore)
talking_point.context: Anchors on a dense Jul 21–22 deal tape against a rallying market — a natural way to open a room-readiness conversation as signings accelerate across sectors.

ranked.read: Prologis makes a "best and final" ~£14B ($18.7B) run at UK REIT SEGRO (JPMorgan, BofA buy-side) — a contested UK-Code cross-border mega-cap.
Vertex takes Crinetics private at $85/share (JPMorgan, Leerink; Lazard, Morgan Stanley), Repligen buys BioLife (Goldman, Perella; Centerview), and Mentari/Dyne raise $200M+/$375M.
NOVAGOLD signs a ~$4.2B all-share consolidation of Donlin Gold (Citi exclusive) — a large JV buy-in with a board fairness opinion.
Corebridge/Equitable merger of equals heads to a Jul 30 vote (Morgan Stanley; Goldman); First Financial acquires Finward (Morgan Stanley buy-side).

pipeline_watch.note: Named covered-bank mandates and sector heat. Rumored / no-adviser situations live in the Rumor Mill.

skeptics_corner.note: Unconfirmed leads — rumored deals and situations with no adviser named yet (or advisers unconfirmed). Real reporting, but treat as speculative: chase them, don't quote them on a call.

items.lead: The Dutch online-auction platform (Permira/Accel-backed) has reportedly hired Barclays to explore options including a potential sale, described as early-stage.
items.why_unverified: Reuters report citing people familiar; no formal process or seller confirmation and the company has not commented.

items.company: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust (NYSE: KREF)
items.lead: The board formed a strategic review committee to evaluate alternatives including a sale, merger, or asset sales, disclosed alongside a $121.8M Q2 net loss.
items.why_unverified: Company-announced review only — no adviser, buyer, or transaction identified, and management will not update until the review concludes.

items.lead: Activist Reference Equity is publicly pressing the board to suspend buybacks, raise equity, and restart an accretive M&A program — a buy-side activist situation.
items.why_unverified: Activist campaign only; the board has not endorsed the strategy and no financing or deal is in motion.

items.lead: Activist ADW Capital (~19.3%) sent a public letter demanding the board internalize its external manager and launch a strategic review including an orderly liquidation, threatening a proxy fight.
items.why_unverified: Activist demand, not a board-endorsed process — CODI has not agreed to any review or sale.

items.lead: The medtech firm is reportedly exploring options including a potential sale after drawing takeover interest from private equity; shares jumped ~10%.
items.why_unverified: Bloomberg report citing people familiar; no PE bidder named on the record and no process confirmed by the company.
